description: Git-versioned tool forge — LLMs create, version, and execute custom tools in isolated worker_threads with Gateway access

tools.name: forge_write
tools.description: Create a new forged tool. Writes source to data/forge/tools/{name}.js, commits to git. The code must export a default async function(args, ctx) where ctx provides { gateway, progress, payload, workspacePath, toolStatePath, args }. Optional args schema for LLM discovery. Optional packages array (checked against allowlist).

description.description: What the tool does — shown in forge_list

code.description: ES module source. Must export default async function(args, ctx).

args.description: Optional args schema: { argName: { type, required, description } }

tools.name: forge_update
tools.description: Update an existing tool's source code. Creates a new git commit — old version stays in history. Use forge_read with a ref to access historical versions.

tools.name: forge_read
tools.description: Read a tool's source code. Pass ref (commit hash) to read a historical version. Without ref, reads the current version.

ref.description: Optional git ref (commit hash) for historical version

tools.name: forge_list
tools.description: List all forged tools (summary form). Pass { name } to get the full manifest including args schema, version, and package status. When packagesPending is true, the tool has unapproved npm packages. To approve, add those packages to config.json agents.forge.allowedPackages (or disable approval by setting requireApprovalForNewPackages=false) and restart the server. Until approved, forge_call will reject with a clear error listing the missing packages.

name.description: Optional: get full manifest for a specific tool

tools.name: forge_delete
tools.description: Soft-delete a tool (removes file + state directory). Commits the deletion. Recoverable from git history via forge_rollback.

tools.name: forge_call
tools.description: Execute a forged tool by name. Resolves payload[] (local path, UNC path, or URL → Buffer[]), spawns a worker_thread, injects context via MessagePort, enforces timeout with worker.terminate(). Returns the tool's return value plus diagnostics (_diagnostics, _logs, _warning, _outputs). Console output is ALWAYS captured and returned in _logs — this is how you debug your own tools. _outputs lists every file the tool wrote to its storagePath, with full retrieval coordinates: {name, path (for storage.read), url (RELATIVE path like /storage/forge/<tool>/<file> — prepend your MCP server origin and fetch with your HTTP tool, e.g. browser_fetch), size, uncPath}. The `uncPath` field (when configured) gives the SMB path for direct access from LAN machines. A _warning field appears when the tool returns undefined/null/empty object (likely a bug). ISOLATION: The forged tool runs in an isolated worker_thread in the server process. It has NO access to chat app tools (chat_archive.*, chat_session.*), NO access to other MCP tools (storage.*, memory.*, browser.*, etc.), and NO ability to make HTTP calls back to the server. It can ONLY use ctx.payload (input Buffers), ctx.gateway (LLM Gateway only), and ctx.storagePath (output dir). If the tool needs data from another source, fetch it at the top level first, save to storage, then pass the storage path as payload. CROSS-MACHINE: payload[] accepts UNC paths (\\server\share\file) — the server resolves them on its own machine. The storage root (config.json agents.storage.root) is often a LAN-mounted share, so forge outputs under <root>\forge\<tool>\* are reachable directly from other machines without going through the HTTP endpoint. MODELS: Pass optional `model` (e.g. 'badkid-llama-chat') to route ALL ctx.gateway.chat() calls inside the tool through that specific Gateway model. Omit to let the Gateway use its default routing (recommended — tool authors should write model-agnostic tools). To discover available models first, call gateway.listModels().

args.description: Arguments object passed to the tool

payload.description: Array of file paths or URLs to resolve to Buffers

model.description: Optional Gateway model identifier (e.g. 'badkid-llama-chat'). Routes all ctx.gateway.chat() calls inside the tool through this model. Omit to use the Gateway's default routing (recommended for compatibility — tool authors should not assume a specific model).

tools.name: forge_history
tools.description: Show git log for a tool (or all tools). Each entry has hash, date, message.

tools.name: forge_rollback
tools.description: Restore a tool to a specific commit. Current state is snapshotted to state/.rollback/{date}/, then state is reset. Creates a new commit — history is never rewritten.

tools.name: forge_help
tools.description: Returns the full tool authoring guide — the ctx API, how to write tools, available gateway methods, payload handling, state persistence patterns, and best practices. Call this before writing your first tool or when unsure about the ctx API.
